description: Add outbound/tunnel proxy support for VPN nodes that are only accessible locally, allowing traffic to route through an external proxy
steps: 1. OUTBOUND CONFIG MODEL: Add outbound proxy configuration to VPN node settings. In the cores config (NodesView.vue), add an 'Outbound' section to each protocol's config form. Fields: outbound_enabled (bool), outbound_type (vless/vmess/trojan/shadowsocks/socks5), outbound_address (server:port), outbound_uuid/password, outbound_tls (bool), outbound_path (for ws), outbound_sni. Store in the vpn_configs extra_json under an 'outbound' key.

2. OUTBOUND UI: In NodesView.vue cores tab, add a collapsible 'Outbound / Tunnel' section below the protocol config for each node. Use the same grouped card pattern as existing config sections. Include a toggle to enable outbound, then show type selector and relevant fields based on selected type.

3. OUTBOUND BACKEND: In panel/internal/api/api.go, ensure the vpn_configs endpoint accepts outbound fields in extra_json. No schema change needed since extra_json is already a JSON blob. When saving, validate outbound fields if enabled.

4. OUTBOUND NODE AGENT: In node/cmd/node/main.go, when applying VPN config, if outbound is configured, generate the appropriate outbound proxy chain. For OpenVPN: add route/redirect-gateway + socks-proxy directive. For IKEv2: use strongswan leftupdown script to route through tunnel. For SSH: use ProxyCommand. Document the approach in the config.

5. OUTBOUND I18N: Add i18n keys for outbound section in admin i18n.ts: nodes.outbound, nodes.outbound_enabled, nodes.outbound_type, nodes.outbound_address, nodes.outbound_uuid, nodes.outbound_tls, nodes.outbound_path, nodes.outbound_sni, nodes.outbound_desc, nodes.hint_outbound - all 3 locales.
6. OUTBOUND DEFAULTS: Add PROTOCOL_DEFAULTS entries for outbound in each protocol: outbound: { enabled: false, type: 'vless', address: '', uuid: '', tls: true, path: '', sni: '' }
acceptance_criteria: Each node protocol config has an 'Outbound / Tunnel' section
Admin can configure outbound proxy type (vless/vmess/trojan/shadowsocks/socks5) with address and credentials
Outbound config is stored in extra_json and persisted via API
Node agent reads outbound config and applies appropriate proxy chain directives
All outbound labels are translated in 3 locales
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/panel/panel/web/admin && npx vue-tsc --noEmit && npx vite build
findings: All builds pass (admin vue-tsc + vite build, portal vue-tsc + vite build, go build for panel and node, go test for both). Outbound config stored in extra_json.outbound nested object - backward compatible since existing configs without this key get defaults merged via spread operator in startEdit(). Russian locale included per user steering.
